---
title: "macOS Client"
description-meta: "Install and use the openme macOS menu-bar app — download, profile setup, and one-click knocking."
keywords: [openme macOS, openme menu bar, openme Mac app, SPA macOS, knock macOS]
---

The openme macOS app lives in the menu bar. Click the icon to knock from any
app without switching windows.

---

## Download {#download}

[Download for macOS →](../download/macos.qmd)

<!-- Screenshot placeholder: macOS menu-bar icon -->
![openme menu bar icon](../images/placeholder-macos-menubar.png){.placeholder}

---

## Install {#install}

1. Download the `.dmg` from the [Download page](../download/macos.qmd).
2. Open the `.dmg` and drag **openme** to `/Applications`.
3. Launch **openme** — it appears in the menu bar.

::: {.callout-note}
macOS may show a security dialog on first launch because the app is not
distributed through the App Store. Open **System Settings → Privacy & Security**
and click **Open Anyway**.
:::

---

## Add a Profile {#profile}

1. Click the openme menu-bar icon.
2. Select **Manage Profiles → Add Profile**.
3. Enter the profile details, or click **Scan QR** and scan the QR code
   generated by `openme add --qr <name>` on the server.

<!-- Screenshot placeholder: Add profile dialog -->
![Add profile dialog](../images/placeholder-macos-add-profile.png){.placeholder}

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| Name | Friendly label for this server (e.g. "Home Server") |
| Server | Hostname or IP of the server |
| Port | UDP knock port (default: `54154`) |
| Server public key | Curve25519 public key — from `openme add` output |
| Your private key | Ed25519 private key — generated by `openme add` |

---

## Send a Knock {#knock}

1. Click the openme menu-bar icon.
2. Click the profile name to knock immediately.

<!-- Screenshot placeholder: Menu showing profile list -->
![Menu-bar knock menu](../images/placeholder-macos-knock.png){.placeholder}

::: {.callout-note}
The app sends a UDP packet and does not wait for a response — the server never
replies. A brief notification confirms the packet was sent.
:::
